title: Application State Machine, Commands, and Recovery · Runtime Lens: Control Projection
summary: Solve can run during generation, pause is a boolean with ambiguous meaning, controls disagree with state, and errors leave the app unusable. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the control projection increment in explicit application transition table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive enabled controls, labels, focus targets, status, and progress from state rather than mutating them independently.
Admit commands through an explicit current-state transition table with effects and rejection feedback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-control-projection — Derive enabled controls, labels, focus targets, status, and progress from state rather than mutating them independently.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-state-machine-controls-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-state-machine-controls-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_state_machine_controls_2(allowed_transitions: int = 18, verified_transitions: int = 18, rejected_commands: int = 9, unsafe_transitions: int = 0, recoverable_failures: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if allowed_transitions < 1 or verified_transitions < 0:
        return False, verified_transitions, "transition-count"
    if verified_transitions != allowed_transitions:
        return False, verified_transitions, "transition-coverage"
    if rejected_commands < 1 or unsafe_transitions > 0:
        return False, unsafe_transitions, "command-admission"
    if recoverable_failures < 1:
        return False, recoverable_failures, "recovery-path"
    assert verified_transitions == allowed_transitions
    return True, verified_transitions, "verified-state-machine"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-control-projection-1bh2v0
# Competency: maze-control-projection.
# Case 5538: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def evidence_maze_control_projection_1bh2v0(allowed_transitions: int = 18, verified_transitions: int = 18, rejected_commands: int = 9, unsafe_transitions: int = 0, recoverable_failures: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1bh2v0 = "1bh2v0-0v2hb1-5538"
    assert evidence_variant_1bh2v0
    evidence_axes_1bh2v0 = (
        "grid1bh2v0",
        "topology5538",
        "search0v2hb1",
        "event1bh2v05538",
        "access55381bh2v0",
        "recovery0v2hb15538",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1bh2v0)) == 6
    if allowed_transitions < 1 or verified_transitions < 0:
        return False, verified_transitions, "transition-count"
    if verified_transitions != allowed_transitions:
        return False, verified_transitions, "transition-coverage"
    if rejected_commands < 1 or unsafe_transitions > 0:
        return False, unsafe_transitions, "command-admission"
    if recoverable_failures < 1:
        return False, recoverable_failures, "recovery-path"
    assert verified_transitions == allowed_transitions
    return True, verified_transitions, "verified-state-machine"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Derive enabled controls, labels, focus targets, status, and progress from state rather than mutating them independently.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Control Projection model · Theory Control Projection, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Control Projection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Control Projection model · Theory Control Projection, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 4499 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to verify explicit application transitions, command rejection, control projection, and recoverable failure states; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.
